summary: P0 login bug verification + Play-Store smoke regression for AiForge. The 'Element type is invalid' crash on /login and /register has been fully resolved — both screens render cleanly, all critical testIDs are present, and there is NO 'Continue with Google' button anywhere on the auth screens (confirmed via grep across login.tsx/register.tsx/_layout.tsx/AuthContext.tsx/api/client.ts + DOM innerText check). BACKEND (8/8 pass in 4.15s via new /app/backend/tests/test_login_regression_iter7.py): POST /api/auth/login (demo → 200 + token; wrong pw → 401), POST /api/auth/register (new → 200+token; duplicate → 400 with 'already registered' detail), GET /api/auth/me with token → 200 (email=demo@example.com), GET /api/auth/me without token → 401, GET /api/plans → exactly 6 plans (free/spark/forge/neon_pro/quantum/singularity), GET /api/creations with token → 200 list, GET /api/admin/me as demo → is_admin=true. FRONTEND (mobile 390x844): /login renders with login-email/login-password/login-submit; email+password (demo@example.com / demo1234) → lands on / (tabs); demo-login one-tap button → lands on / (tabs); page reload after login → session persists (still on /, not bounced to /login); /register renders and reg-name/reg-email/reg-password/register-submit all present; brand-new TEST_iter7_<uuid>@example.com registration → tabs; re-submitting the same email → NO crash, stays on /register (backend correctly returns 400; the RN Alert.alert is a no-op on web preview — known web-only limitation per iter-6 notes); tabs show Home/Library/Plans/Profile/Create; Profile shows demo@example.com + FORGE PLAN + daily-limit meter (1/200) + referral card; /cad, /avatar, /editor screens all render without crash (headers 'AVATAR MAKER', '3D / CAD GENERATOR', 'EDITOR' confirmed). Zero pageerrors and zero console errors other than the single expected 400 from the intentional duplicate-email test. Play Store readiness: GREEN.

issues: React Native Alert.alert for 'Email already registered' does not render visible UI in the Expo web preview (RN-Web no-op). The user stays on /register with no visible feedback on web. On native Android/iOS this Alert IS rendered by the OS, so it is NOT a Play Store blocker — but if a web build is ever shipped, consider replacing Alert.alert with an inline error banner for consistency.

action_items: (Optional, polish only) Replace Alert.alert with an inline error banner on /login and /register so the same error UX works on both native and web builds. Currently Alert on RN-Web is a no-op, so the 'Email and password don't match' / 'Email already registered' messages are invisible to web-preview users (native is fine — Play Store scope). Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: login.tsx and register.tsx are clean — lucide-react-native imports are limited to icons that actually exist in v1.17.0 (Mail, Lock, Sparkles / Mail, Lock, User, Gift). The regression that broke rendering (importing a non-existent `Chrome` icon) is gone.

_layout.tsx no longer references GoogleSessionCatcher — Stack renders <index>, <onboarding>, <(auth)>, <(tabs)>, <creation/[id]>, <editor>, <cad>, <avatar>, <legal/privacy>, <legal/terms>, <admin/secrets>, <payment/success>. Clean.

AuthContext.tsx exposes signIn / signUp / signOut / refresh — no signInWithGoogleSession. api/client.ts has no googleAuth() method. src/utils/ directory contains only `storage` (googleAuth.ts is gone). Frontend is fully email/password.

Backend /api/auth/google endpoint is still defined server-side but is dead code from the frontend's perspective — flagged as intentional by main agent and confirmed no frontend reference. Safe to leave, or remove in a future cleanup for a smaller attack surface.
seed_data_creation: One throwaway user created for the register→duplicate flow: TEST_iter7_<uuid>@example.com / test1234. No persistent state changes. demo@example.com credentials verified unchanged (still demo1234, still admin, still on forge plan).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The 'Element type is invalid' crash from the phantom `Chrome` icon import is fully resolved. If any future iteration touches lucide-react-native icons, ALWAYS grep the installed version (currently 1.17.0 in package.json) — importing a name that doesn't exist returns `undefined`, and using `undefined` as a JSX element crashes ALL screens that import that module. Preferred safe icons already in use: Mail, Lock, Sparkles, User, Gift, Home, Library, Plus, ChevronRight, etc. For full regression run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v --tb=short --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml` (73 previous + 8 new = ~81 tests). For frontend, mobile viewport 390x844 with testIDs login-email/login-password/login-submit/demo-login/reg-email/reg-password/register-submit is the critical smoke set.
rca_of_issue: RCA (already fixed by main agent, confirmed by this test pass): The prior 'Continue with Google' button imported `Chrome` from `lucide-react-native@1.17.0` — that icon name does not exist in the installed version, so the import evaluated to `undefined`. React Native then threw 'Element type is invalid: expected a string or a class/function but got: undefined' when the login/register modules loaded, which crashed the ENTIRE screen (not just the button). Fix applied: main agent removed all Google-auth frontend code (the Chrome icon and Google button from login.tsx/register.tsx, GoogleSessionCatcher from _layout.tsx, signInWithGoogleSession from AuthContext.tsx, googleAuth from api/client.ts, and deleted src/utils/googleAuth.ts). Verification (this iteration): grep confirms no Chrome/Google/GoogleSession references remain in the reference files; Playwright confirms /login and /register render, email/password + demo-login both succeed, session persists, and downstream tabs + /cad + /avatar + /editor routes all render.
